We first went to Ceasar's Palace in 1966 on our honeymoon ... the first casino experience for either of us, and the beginning of a half-century of fulfilled high expectations. We rode out the transition from the mob to the MBA's and the growing pains of aquisition expansion that followed, adapting from free-wheeling pit boss gratuities to structured Total Rewards procedures, becoming Diamond members for decades. We always felt we were getting what we had paid for ... Maybe.even more, occasionally. Until. 2020. At first we blamed the decline in service & the employee bad attitudes on Covid 19, but we have come to realize that it flows downhill , from new owners who are trying to squeeze every cent out of the old Ceasar's. Gone are the Diamond Lounges, the monthly Diamond free shows, the Diamond promotions, the room upgrades, ... even a comp room is tough to schedule now. Shame on you , El Dorado management, for gutting a Vegas tradition! Update:. No communications received from Caesars. Their apparent response is to ignore our critique. Well, looks like we'll be going to MGM properties from now on. Reduced to 1 star!

I am a slot player and I understand the odds, entertainment only purposes, etc., etc., etc., however when you walk in the door and you can drop over $2k within 30 minutes and you might hit $1200 as a jackpot if you are one of the “Lucky Ones” then there has to be something terribly wrong with this picture.  

Let’s talk drawings, I have seen the same people win free play however I have yet to ever win a drawing however I don’t seem to have that problem at other casinos in the area.  

It is just so overwhelming disheartening to sit and watch the same people win, all the time and win and win and win when you are spending donating the same amount of money to your establishment as all the others!  I mean when you watch someone hit 2 different slot machines 8 times between the 2 and you hit 1 small once you walk in the door so that you not only play it but another $3k on top of it well you get the picture!  

Have I ever had an invitation to  a Noir account even though my tier score is well over 250k and my husband’s is over 75k (same bank account and household)) nope…this is the norm.  Have I ever had a Slot Host contact me Nope.  I have for other establishments that I have spent a whole lot less with. 

I’m just tired of hearing everyone talk about all their “Big Winnings” when I have NEVER had this happen with you!

I think it’s time for me to seriously consider taking my money elsewhere because quite frankly no one can continue to throw money out the window. 

I hate it!
Regards,
